spec_match.rationale: Mirrors `navigator.crashReporter.set(key, value)` semantics with an attached-to-report buffer — matches the spec's per-Document key-value map.

interactive.rationale: Key/value inputs, set button, table of active pairs, simulate-fault trigger producing a wire-shape payload.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb promises add metadata then simulate; the page delivers exactly that, including the payload preview.

edge_cases.rationale: Doesn't surface the per-Document size budget the spec mentions, value length truncation, or what happens when you set a key that's already set (overwrite vs append).

references.rationale: Includes the correct chromestatus.com/feature/6228675846209536 link in see-also.

design_system.rationale: Variables only, the th cells use --text-black/--bg-ivory pair correctly, no static-card.

openQuestions.title: Show budget / truncation behaviour
openQuestions.detail: The spec discussion calls out a per-Document map with size limits. Add a stress button that sets 100 keys of 1KB each and surfaces the truncation policy — useful for SRE planning what to store.

openQuestions.title: Demonstrate delete + overwrite
openQuestions.detail: `set(key, null)` (delete) and re-setting an existing key are both first-class operations. The page only shows the set path; add a delete button per row so the model is complete.

summary: Solid baseline that captures the spec's API shape and the wire payload visually. Edge-case coverage is still shallow.
